page: Hetzner Cloud cax41 is an ARM64-based virtual server powered by the QEMU hypervisor. It features 16 shared vCPUs utilizing the Ampere Altra Neoverse-N1 processor architecture running at 2.0 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 31.0 GB of system memory, providing approximately 1.95 GB of RAM per core. Local storage is bundled with a 320 GB SSD, and there are zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses included. No dedicated GPU hardware is present.

Benchmark evaluations indicate average multi-core performance, with a stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 29117.84 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 8498.0. Single-core performance is relatively weaker, as evidenced by a Geekbench single-core score of 1034.0 and a poor single-threaded bzip3 decompression rate of 7.08 GB/sec. Memory bandwidth is measured at 1126.26 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 102.2 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. Cryptographic performance is limited, with a PassMark CPU encryption score of 2590.29, though the processor natively supports AES instructions.

The server demonstrates average capabilities in database operations, achieving 22.71 million operations per second in Redis SET tests and a PassMark database score of 10852.79. Static web serving throughput is solid at 16.72 GB/sec. However, the instance is poorly suited for large language model tasks, with a weak prompt processing speed of 18.92 tokens per second on a medium 7B model. Given its shared ARM64 architecture and high core density relative to memory, the cax41 offers a cost-efficient profile for multi-threaded workloads such as software compilation, web serving, and general-purpose application hosting that do not require dedicated CPU resources.
description: Hetzner Cloud cax41 is an ARM64 virtual server featuring 16 shared vCPUs on the Ampere Altra Neoverse-N1 architecture running at 2.0 GHz. It is equipped with 31.0 GB of RAM and a local 320 GB SSD, with no integrated GPU. Benchmarks show average multi-core performance, highlighted by a Geekbench multi-core score of 8498.0 and a stress-ng multi-core score of 29117.84, though single-core metrics remain weak. The server delivers a solid static web serving throughput of 16.72 GB/sec and handles database operations reliably, as shown by 22.71 million Redis SET operations per second. Due to its shared CPU allocation and 1:2 memory-to-vCPU ratio, this instance represents a cost-efficient option for multi-threaded workloads, software compilation, and web serving, while being less suitable for intensive cryptographic or single-threaded tasks.
og_description: Hetzner Cloud cax41 is an ARM64 virtual server featuring 16 shared Ampere Altra vCPUs, 31.0 GB of RAM, and a 320 GB SSD, offering balanced multi-core performance for web serving and multi-threaded workloads.
meta_description: Hetzner Cloud cax41 is an ARM64 virtual server with 16 shared vCPUs, 31.0 GB RAM, and a 320 GB SSD, optimized for cost-efficient multi-threaded tasks.
tagline: An ARM64 virtual server with sixteen shared cores and local SSD storage, optimized for multi-threaded workloads and web serving.

31.0 GB of system memory and 320 GB of local SSD storage
Average multi-core performance with 8498.0 Geekbench multi-core score
Static web serving throughput of 16.72 GB/sec
Best suited for multi-threaded applications, software compilation, and web hosting
